title: trifolium pratense convar. serotinum
intro: Trifolium pratense convar. serotinum, also known as Red Clover, is a legume plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, primarily due to its rich content of isoflavones, which exhibit estrogenic activity. The medicinal properties of Red Clover include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as alleviating menopausal symptoms, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting bone density. Traditionally, Red Clover is prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in herbal infusions, often combined with other medicinal plants.
